title: The Encoding — Fluid Computation in Detail

analysis.object: T942: The Encoding — Fluid Computation in Detail depth analysis

turing_machine_components.description: UTM components: tape (infinite, binary), head (read/write/move), state (finite), transition function. All must be encoded in NS flow.

tape_as_vortex_array.description: Tape cell k: vortex ring at height z=k*Delta with orientation encoding the bit. Delta >> eta (above Kolmogorov scale). Infinite tape = infinite height (physical idealization or periodic with long period).
tape_as_vortex_array.reason: Tape = vertical array of vortex rings

head_as_vortex_ring.description: Head = special vortex ring of larger radius R >> r. The head ring moves up/down by interacting with tape rings. Move right = amplify upward drift. Move left = amplify downward drift.
head_as_vortex_ring.reason: Head = large vortex ring; move by induction

state_in_flow_pattern.description: State = flow pattern of the head ring. Finite many states = finite many ring configurations (radius, circulation, pitch). Encoded in EML-0 (finite discrete structure).

transition_via_interaction.description: Transition function: when head ring reaches a tape ring, they interact via Biot-Savart. The interaction reads the tape bit (orientation), changes the tape bit (write), and moves the head.

eml_traversal.description: The complete encoding: EML-0 (bits, states) -> EML-1 (gate interactions) -> EML-2 (memory stability) -> EML-3 (clock = periodic ring shedding). The full EML ladder. Self-reference at EML-inf via proof verifier encoding.

t942_theorem.description: T942: Explicit encoding of UTM in 3D NS. Bits = vortex rings (EML-0). Gates = Biot-Savart interactions (EML-1). Memory = stable tori (EML-2). Clock = periodic shedding (EML-3). Self-reference = proof verifier encoding (EML-inf). The EML ladder is fully traversed. T942.
t942_theorem.reason: Fluid encoding: explicit UTM; EML-0 through EML-inf

analysis.theorem: T942: The Encoding — Fluid Computation in Detail (S1222).

key_theorem: T942: The Encoding — Fluid Computation in Detail (S1222).
